_readme: External reference links shown as an 'Other links' group at the bottom of the Mod Docs tab. Everything else in that tab is a doc file read off the box out of an @mod folder; these are the off-box sources those mods come from, kept here so the answer to 'where is this documented?' lives next to the docs themselves instead of in someone's bookmarks. Add a link = add one row. Only http/https URLs are rendered - anything else is dropped by the UI. This file ships with the ConfigViewer deploy (static asset, no API involvement), so adding a link needs no Api redeploy.

links.name: DayZ Expansion - Scripts
links.note: Source for the @expansion* mods. The authority on Expansion settings files and their fields.

links.name: DayZ Expansion - Missions
links.note: Per-map Expansion mission templates. Our expansion_types.xml / expansion_spawnabletypes.xml come from here.

links.name: VPP Admin Tools
links.note: Source for @vppadmintools - permissions, SuperAdmins.txt, the server-status webhook that feeds our FPS metric.

links.name: VPP Admin Tools - Wiki
links.note: Setup and permission-group reference for VPP.

links.name: DayZ Modding Wiki (StarDZ)
links.note: Community wiki - Enforce Script, config files, server admin. Broad but self-described pre-alpha and AI-assisted; verify before acting on it.

links.name: dzconfig - serverDZ.cfg
links.note: Field-by-field serverDZ.cfg reference. The one to check before adding a parameter to server-settings.json.

links.name: dzconfig - Wiki index
links.note: types.xml, events, spawnabletypes and the rest of the Central Economy.
